Title: TaTPP-7A positively feedback regulates grain filling and wheat grain yield through T6P-SnRK1 signalling pathway and sugar–ABA interaction

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Fielder; Zhongyou9507; Hongjinmai; Chinese Spring; Kronos background for mutant support
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Chinese landraces and modern cultivars; Chinese Spring reference
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing grain endosperm; aleurone-associated developing grain tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Specifically and highly expressed in developing grains, especially 10–20 DPA; enriched in aleurone layer; also expressed in young spikes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in developing grains/filling stage, higher in Hap I high-TKW varieties than Hap II low-TKW varieties
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Trehalose-6-phosphate phosphatase (TPP); IIB subfamily of HAD superfamily; conserved TPP domains
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; BSR-seq; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; wheat overexpression; RNAi; CRISPR/Cas9 editing of TaTPPs; Kronos mutant analysis; metabolite profiling; starch granule SEM; promoter activity assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes grain filling, grain size, thousand-kernel weight, and yield; lowers amylose content and amylose/amylopectin ratio; alters starch physicochemical properties; promotes earlier maturity
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced amylose content and altered starch gelatinization/pasting properties may affect processing quality; CRISPR/mutant reduction of TaTPPs decreases grain number, spikelet number, spike length, and yield
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaTPP-7A was identified by GWAS plus BSR-seq as the major 7AS candidate underlying thousand-kernel weight. 2. It is specifically expressed in developing grains and highly expressed in the aleurone layer during filling. 3. Overexpression increases TKW, grain length, filling rate, and yield, while RNAi and mutants reduce these traits. 4. TaTPP-7A affects sucrose lysis/transport, starch synthesis, amylose proportion, and grain maturation through T6P-SnRK1 signalling and sugar–ABA/ethylene interaction. 5. Favorable haplotypes were selected during domestication and breeding.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for wheat yield improvement and grain-filling enhancement; favorable Hap I is useful for marker-assisted selection; haplotype choice may help adaptation/escape under warming climates; trehalose-pathway engineering is recommended with attention to starch quality shifts
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field trials across multiple environments/years; grain developmental stages 5, 10, 15, 20 DPA
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Exome sequencing PRJNA550304; BSR-seq PRJNA879906; RNA-seq PRJNA882584
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primary focal gene of the paper; acts in T6P signalling and is framed as a domestication- and breeding-targeted gene. Although enzymatic, the paper emphasizes system-level regulation of grain filling and yield, so categorized here as Common Gene per transporter/storage/other non-TF/non-explicit pathway-control context.
